---
description: "Use when adding environment variables, config options, or modifying .example.env. Enforces the generated .example.env pattern."
applyTo: "packages/web/src/lib/server/config.ts, packages/web/src/lib/server/config.defaults.ts, .example.env"
---

# Environment Variable & Config Instructions

## Rule: `.example.env` is generated — never edit it manually

`.example.env` is generated by `generateExampleDotenvFile()` in
`packages/web/src/lib/server/config.defaults.ts`. Editing `.example.env` directly will be
overwritten the next time the generator runs.

**When adding a new environment variable:**

1. Add the default value or placeholder to `defaultConfigValues` in `config.defaults.ts` (if applicable).
2. Add the variable — commented or uncommented — to the template string inside `generateExampleDotenvFile()` in `config.defaults.ts`.
3. Add the parsing logic to `getPenombreConfig()` in `config.ts`.
4. Run `bun run --filter @penombre/web gen:env` to regenerate `.example.env`.
5. Do **not** touch `.example.env` directly.

## Docker-level variables

Some variables (e.g. `GARAGE_RPC_SECRET`) are consumed by Docker services, not by the app
itself. They still belong in `generateExampleDotenvFile()` as commented-out entries with a
description, so users have a single reference file for all required variables.
